summary: Fix correctly restores Accept-Language rewriting for all domains. The two-line removal of the is_target_domain() guard from request() is minimal and surgical. JS injection and CSP nonce logic in response() remain correctly gated behind is_target_domain(). Tests adequately cover the fix with both a renamed existing test and a new TestAcceptLanguageAllDomains class.

suggestions.suggestion: TestAcceptLanguageAllDomains duplicates FakeHeaders/FakeRequest/FakeFlow infrastructure from TestGeoFixAddon's make_flow fixture. Could extract to a shared module-level fixture or conftest.
suggestions.benefit: Reduces test code duplication and maintenance surface.

fix_verified.evidence: request() no longer has is_target_domain() guard. Lines 146-151 of src/proxy_addon.py: lock acquired, _last_flow_time updated, accept_lang read from preset, header set unconditionally for every flow.
fix_verified.lock_scope_note: Lock correctly covers _last_flow_time and _preset reads. Header assignment outside lock is safe — flow is not shared between threads.

js_csp_guards_intact.evidence: src/proxy_addon.py:158-161: response() still returns early if not is_target_domain(host). No change to JS injection or CSP nonce logic.

tests_cover_fix.renamed_test: test_request_rewrites_accept_language_non_target_domain — assertion flipped from 'ru-RU' (no rewrite) to 'en-US,en;q=0.9' (rewrite applied). Correctly validates fix.
tests_cover_fix.new_test_class: TestAcceptLanguageAllDomains: 3 tests — non-target (example.com), random (some-random-site.org), and target (www.google.com). All verify Accept-Language is rewritten.
